info.title: Data Governance API
info.description:
The **Data Governance API** provides a set of endpoints for managing the lifecycle of
entity data within the epilot platform. It enables organizations to define governance
policies — such as automated data deletion rules — and execute them against any entity
schema (currently limited to Contacts).

## Core Concepts

### Entity Lifecycle Configs
A lifecycle **config** is a reusable governance policy that defines:
- Which entities to target (via a saved view and optional filters)
- What action to perform (currently `deletion`)
- When and how often to run (via a schedule)

Lifecycle configs can be enabled or disabled and are evaluated on a recurring schedule.

### Jobs
A **Job** represents a single execution run of a lifecycle config. Jobs track progress,
record outcomes (matched, deleted, failed counts), and can produce downloadable
CSV reports. Jobs may be triggered on a schedule or manually.

### Query
The query endpoint allows previewing which entities match a governance policy
before it is executed, combining a saved view with additional data governance
filters (e.g., "no related entities", "workflows all closed").

## Authentication
All endpoints require a valid epilot OAuth2 bearer token passed in the
`Authorization` header. Optionally, the `x-epilot-org-id` header can be used
to target a specific organization for shared-tenant access.

tags.name: Query
tags.description:
Preview which entities match a governance policy by combining a saved view
with additional data governance filters.

tags.name: Lifecycle Configs
tags.description:
Create, update, list, and retrieve governance policy configurations that
define automated actions (e.g., deletion) on entity data.

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description:
Create, list, retrieve, and update job runs that represent individual
executions of a governance config. Includes report download.

post.summary: Query entities matching a governance policy
post.description:
Executes a query against the specified entity schema using a saved view
definition, optionally combined with additional data governance filters.

This endpoint is typically used to **preview** which entities would be
affected by a governance config before it runs. The response includes
the total hit count and (optionally hydrated) entity results.

Pagination is supported via `from` and `size` in the request body.

parameters.name: entity_schema
parameters.description:
The target entity schema slug to query
(e.g. `contact`, `opportunity`, `order`).

200.description: Successfully executed query. Returns matching entities and total hit count.

400.description: Invalid request — malformed filters or missing saved view ID.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — caller lacks permission to query the specified schema.

post.description:
Creates a new job run for the given entity schema. The job is associated
with an existing config and records the scheduled execution date.

This is a low-level endpoint used internally by the scheduler. For
manually triggering a config execution, prefer
`POST /data-management/v1/configs/{config_id}/jobs`.

parameters.name: entity_schema
parameters.description: The entity schema slug this job operates on (e.g. `contact`).

patch.description:
Partially updates an existing job run. Typically used to record
progress or finalize a job by setting its status to `success` or
`failed`, attaching details, error messages, or a report reference.

parameters.name: entity_schema
parameters.description: The entity schema slug this job belongs to.

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the job to update.

get.description:
Returns full details of a single job run, including its current status,
execution timestamps, type-specific details, and report reference
(if available).

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the job.

get.summary: Get report download URL for a job
get.description:
Returns a short-lived, pre-signed S3 URL to download the CSV report
file for the given job. The URL expires after the number of seconds
indicated in the `expires_in` field.

A report is only available after a job has completed. If the job is
still in progress or did not produce a report, a 404 is returned.

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the job whose report to download.

404.description: Job not found or report not yet available.

get.description:
Returns a single data governance config by its unique identifier,
including its query definition, schedule, and current enabled state.

parameters.name: config_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the config.

post.summary: Trigger a manual job run for a config
post.description:
Manually triggers a new job run for the specified config. The job is
created and queued for asynchronous execution.

Returns the newly created job, whose `id` can be used to poll status
via `GET /data-management/v1/jobs/{job_id}`.

The job's `trigger` field will be set to `manual` and `triggered_by`
will contain the authenticated user's identifier.

parameters.name: config_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the config to execute.

201.description: Job created and queued for execution.

post.summary: Create or update a governance config
post.description:
Creates a new governance config or updates an existing one for the
given entity schema. The config defines:
- A **query** (saved view + optional filters) to identify target entities
- A **schedule** controlling how often the policy runs
- An **action type** (currently only `deletion`)

Once created and enabled, the config is evaluated on its schedule by a
background process that creates job runs automatically.

Returns `201` when a new config is created, `200` when an existing
config is updated.

parameters.name: entity_schema
parameters.description: The entity schema slug this config targets (e.g. `contact`).

get.description:
Returns a cursor-paginated list of governance configs. Results can be
filtered by entity schema, config type, scheduled run date, or
enabled status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of configs to return per page.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned from a previous response for fetching the
next page of results.

parameters.name: entity_schema
parameters.description: Filter configs by entity schema slug (e.g. `contact`).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter configs by governance action type.

parameters.name: next_run_at
parameters.description:
Filter configs whose next scheduled run date matches this value
(format: `YYYY-MM-DD`).

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Filter by enabled (`true`) or disabled (`false`) status.

get.description:
Returns a cursor-paginated list of job runs. Results can be filtered
by entity schema, action type, execution status, or parent config.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to return per page.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned from a previous response for fetching the
next page of results.

parameters.name: entity_schema
parameters.description: Filter jobs by entity schema slug (e.g. `contact`).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter jobs by governance action type.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter jobs by execution status.

parameters.name: config_id
parameters.description: Filter jobs belonging to a specific config.

EpilotAuth.description: Authorization header with epilot OAuth2 bearer token

EpilotOrg.description: Overrides the target organization to allow shared tenant access
EpilotOrg.name: x-epilot-org-id

ConfigType.description:
The governance action type. Determines what operation is performed on
matched entities when a job runs. Currently only `deletion` is supported.

JobStatus.description:
Current execution status of a job run.
- `in_progress` — the job is actively processing entities.
- `success` — the job completed without critical errors.
- `failed` — the job terminated due to an error.

JobTrigger.description:
Indicates how the job was initiated.
- `schedule` — automatically created by the background scheduler.
- `manual` — explicitly triggered by a user via the API.

JobReportFormat.description: File format of the job report. Currently only CSV is supported.

JobReport.description:
Reference to a report file stored in S3 that details the outcome of a
job run (e.g., which entities were deleted or failed).

bucket.description: S3 bucket where the report file is stored.

key.description: S3 object key of the report file.

CreateJobRequest.description: Request payload for creating a new job run.

config_id.description: ID of the governance config this job executes.

scheduled_for.description: The date this job is scheduled to process (format `YYYY-MM-DD`).

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp marking when execution began.

JobDetails.description:
Type-specific job outcome payload. The shape depends on the config type.
For `deletion` jobs, typical fields include:
- `matched_count` — total entities matched by the query
- `deleted_count` — entities successfully deleted
- `failed_count` — entities that could not be deleted

UpdateJobRequest.description:
Partial update payload for an existing job. Only the fields provided
will be merged into the job record.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp marking when the job finished.

error.description: Human-readable error message if the job failed.

Job.description:
Represents a single execution run of a governance config. Tracks the
full lifecycle from creation through completion, including outcome
details and an optional downloadable report.

config_id.description: ID of the governance config this job was created from.

entity_schema.description: Entity schema slug this job operates on.

scheduled_for.description: The date this job was scheduled to process (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

error.description: Error message if the job failed.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the job record was created.

last_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent update to this job.

triggered_by.description:
Identifier of the user who triggered the job. Set when
`trigger` is `manual`; `null` for scheduled jobs.

ListJobsResponse.description: Paginated response containing a list of job runs.

jobs.description: Array of job records for the current page.

cursor.description:
Opaque cursor for fetching the next page. `null` when there are
no more results.

JobReportUrlResponse.description: Contains a time-limited pre-signed URL to download a job report.

url.description: Pre-signed S3 URL for downloading the report file.

expires_in.description: Number of seconds until the pre-signed URL expires.

QueryFilterType.description:
Predefined data governance filter types that can be layered on top of
a saved view to narrow down target entities:
- `entity_workflows_only_in_closed_or_cancelled_status` — include only
  entities whose own workflows are all in a closed/cancelled state.
- `no_related_entities` — include only entities with no related entities
  of the specified schemas.
- `related_entities_all_in_closed_or_cancelled_status` — include only
  entities whose related entities are all closed or cancelled.
- `related_entities_workflows_only_in_closed_or_cancelled_status` —
  include only entities whose related entities' workflows are all
  closed or cancelled.
- `no_email_communication_since` — include only entities with no email
  communication (sent or received) within the lookback period.

QueryFilter.description:
A single governance filter condition applied during entity querying.
The required and optional fields depend on the `type`.

related_entity_schemas.description:
Entity schema slugs to consider when evaluating relationship-based
filters (e.g. `no_related_entities`).

lookback_period_days.description:
Number of days to look back when evaluating time-based filters
such as `no_email_communication_since`.

message_type.description:
Email message direction(s) to consider. Applicable to
`no_email_communication_since`.

workflow_status.description: Workflow statuses considered "terminal" for workflow-based filters.

QueryConfig.description:
Defines the query used by a governance config to identify target
entities. Combines a saved view with optional governance filters.

saved_view_id.description: ID of the saved view that provides the base entity query.

include_deleted.description:
Controls whether soft-deleted entities are included:
- `true` — include both active and deleted entities
- `false` — exclude deleted entities (default)
- `only` — return only deleted entities

filters.description:
Additional data governance filters layered on top of the saved view
to further narrow the set of matched entities.

QueryEntitiesRequest.description:
Request body for the query endpoint. Extends `QueryConfig` with
pagination and projection options.

size.description: Maximum number of results to return.

hydrate.description:
When `true`, return full entity payloads. When `false` (default),
return only entity IDs and minimal metadata.

fields.description:
List of entity attribute names to include in the response.
Acts as a projection to reduce payload size.

QueryEntitiesResult.description: Response from the entity query endpoint.

hits.description: Total number of entities matching the query.

results.description:
Array of matched entity objects. Shape depends on the `hydrate`
and `fields` options in the request.

ConfigSchedule.description: Schedule definition controlling when a governance config runs.

IntervalConfigSchedule.description:
Interval-based schedule. The governance engine will create a job every
`interval_days` days, optionally bounded by start and end dates.

frequency.description: Schedule type. Currently only `interval` is supported.

interval_days.description: Number of days between consecutive job runs.

start_date.description:
Earliest date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) the schedule is active. If omitted,
the schedule starts immediately.

end_date.description:
Latest date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) the schedule is active. If omitted,
the schedule runs indefinitely.

UpsertConfigRequest.description: Request payload for creating or updating a governance config.

relations_for_deletion.description:
Entity schemas whose related entities should also be deleted
when the primary entity is removed. Only applicable when `type`
is `deletion`.

enabled.description: Whether this config is active and should be evaluated on schedule.

Config.description:
A governance config defining an automated policy (e.g., scheduled
entity deletion) for a specific entity schema.

type.description: Governance action type. Currently only `deletion` is supported.

entity_schema.description: Entity schema slug this config targets.

enabled.description: Whether this config is currently active.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the config was created.

last_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent update.

next_run_at.description:
Next scheduled run date (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Computed from the schedule
after each job run.

relations_for_deletion.description:
Related entity schemas whose entities will also be deleted
alongside the primary entity.

last_run_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent job run.

DeletionRelationEntitySchema.description:
Entity schema slug that can be specified as a cascading deletion
target. When a primary entity is deleted, related entities of these
schemas are also removed.

ListConfigsResponse.description: Paginated response containing a list of governance configs.

configs.description: Array of config records for the current page.

cursor.description:
Opaque cursor for fetching the next page. `null` when there are
no more results.
